semantic_journey: Arabic kasara = to break, crush, shatter. Greek kresera = a flour sieve. Breaking grain and sieving grain are sequential stages of grain-processing. 2-step material-culture: breaking-grain -> grinding -> sieving-the-result.
key_insight: Arabic breaking/crushing and Greek flour-sieve are sequential stages of the same grain-processing operation.

semantic_journey: Arabic dukhan = smoke. Greek andikha = asunder, in twain (directional adverb). Smoke dispersing and the directional asunder-adverb share no semantic path. Greek andikha encodes two-way separation, not general dispersion.
key_insight: Arabic smoke/dispersing and Greek asunder-adverb (two-way separation) share no plausible semantic path.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-shafaq = compassionate caution, tender fear/concern. Greek dialysiphilou = of the love-dissolving. Protective-vigilance and love-dissolving are in different domains and actually opposite in sentiment. No valid path.
key_insight: Arabic compassionate caution and Greek love-dissolving are in different domains and semantically opposite.

semantic_journey: Arabic dakhala = to enter, go into an interior. Greek dolikhaylos = long-tubed (dolikhos=long + aylos=pipe/tube). Core: interior passage. 2-step drift: entering-interior -> interior-passage -> long-interior-tube.
key_insight: Arabic entering-an-interior and Greek long-tubed share the concept of an interior passage, with Greek specializing to a long tube.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-qanaab = greedy, ravenous. Greek pelekan = pelican. Greek pelekanos has its own etymology (pelekus=axe, from beak shape). The reasoning is circular (greedy -> pelican as big-eater -> pelican). No valid path.
key_insight: Arabic greedy/ravenous and Greek pelican share big-eater image, but Greek pelekanos has its own axe-beak etymology.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-katad = groups or clustered masses. Greek telikaytai = so great, of such magnitude. Clusters and magnitude-adjective so-great are in different categories requiring 3+ steps: clusters -> many -> so-many -> so-great.
key_insight: Arabic clustered-masses and Greek so-great magnitude are in different categories with no valid 2-step path.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-lamat = took away, went off with. Greek leleimmetha = we have been left (perfect passive of leipo). Both sides of a leaving event (taker vs remaindee). Negation-cognate possible but phonetics (l-m-t vs l-p) are too distant to support.
key_insight: Arabic going-off-with and Greek being-left-behind are opposite sides of leaving; negation-cognate insufficient with distant phonetics.

semantic_journey: Arabic qisma = division, apportionment (dividing-and-allotting portions). Greek diakosmeo = to divide and marshal in order, to arrange systematically. Core: division and ordered arrangement. 1-step drift: dividing-and-allotting -> dividing-and-marshaling-in-order.
key_insight: Arabic division/apportionment and Greek dividing-and-marshaling share systematic division and ordered allotment.
